chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI S.M.A. No. 320 of 2019 Date Order with signature of Judge For hearing of main petition. 06.11.2019 Ms. Shahida Jatoi, advocate for petitioner. ----------- The learned counsel for the petitioner states that in Schedule of Property description of property has not been fully described; therefore, she seeks permission for making correction. Permission is granted. Through the instant petition, Petitioner Rashid Ali has prayed for the grant of Letter of Administration in respect of immoveable property/assets, details whereof are mentioned in the Schedule of Property attached with the memo of petition, available at page 21, which was left by his deceased mother, namely, Suriya Wasif Ali Wd/o Wasif Ali, who allegedly died on 20.01.2018 at Karachi, leaving behind (i) Rashid Ali (son/petitioner), (ii) Riffat Ali (son) and (iii) Aisha Ali (daughter) as her legal heirs. Original Death Certificate of deceased as well as photostat copies of Family Registration Certificate, issued by the NADRA and title documents of the immovable properties are also filed at page 11 to 19 and 25 to 35. Legal heirs at serial No.2&3 have filed their affidavits of no objection for the grant of Letter of Administration in favour of the petitioner. Two witnesses, namely, Sameer Ali S/o Rashid Ali and Syed Hamza Raza S/o Syed Nayyar Iqbal Raza have also filed their 2 respective affidavits and affirmed that there is no other legal heir of the deceased except mentioned above. Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 08.10.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 24.10.2019.

Their affidavits are also available on record. Notice of this petition was published in Daily “Jang” Karachi dated 08.10.2019; however, none has filed any objection to the grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, as per the report submitted by the Deputy Registrar (OS) on 24.10.2019. Today above named legal heirs and witnesses are present. They affirm the contents of their affidavits and also verify their signatures thereon. Since all the legal formalities/requirements have been fulfilled, there is no impediment in grant of Letter of Administration to petitioner, therefore, the petition is allowed. The office is directed to issue Letter of Administration in respect of immovable properties/assets shown in the Schedule of Properties, as per rules. J U D G E Abrar
